what_it_does: Defines a static phone-sized home/workshop screen with a status bar, greeting, stats band, two summary plates, a morning activity list, count strip, primary action, and bottom icon rail; hard-coded data is rendered into rows and icons through a DNA.org.icon helper.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts, plus /surface/styles/piece.css and /surface/styles/phone.css.
Adds inline CSS for body padding and a two-column .row2 layout.
Displays workshop-themed metrics: days in order, seams open, pieces kept, and hands in.
Injects two header icons, five activity rows, and five rail icons with inline JavaScript.
Uses hard-coded activity row text, timestamps, and dot colors.
standouts: The screen reads as a compact dashboard with ritual/workshop language throughout.
The bottom rail and primary action make the mockup feel like an app screen rather than a static report.
Color and icon variation are encoded directly in the row and rail rendering data.
conventions: Uses a single HTML file with embedded style and script for the view-specific layer.
Relies on shared CSS variables and component class names such as p-band, p-bs, p-plate, p-row, and p-rail.
Keeps content data local as JavaScript arrays and maps it into HTML strings.
Uses IDs only as script injection targets.
concerns: The file assumes DNA.org.icon is available after loading /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
innerHTML is used for all injected icon and row markup, though the data is local and hard-coded.
Several visual labels and counts are duplicated as static content rather than derived from one source.
notes: A self-contained v1 mockup of a workshop-themed mobile home screen driven by shared surface styles and a small runtime icon helper.
questions: What interaction, if any, is expected from the primary action and bottom rail?
Are the counts intended to correspond to the hard-coded rows or remain independent display copy?
Should the workshop language map to a real domain model or stay as atmospheric UI text?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and path indicate a home/bench screen, and the file implements a mobile dashboard-like screen for that purpose.
intention_signals: It seems intended to present a polished home screen concept where a keeper can see current workshop status, morning activity, and a primary next action.
novelty: Uses tactile craft vocabulary such as seams, cuts, tide, kiln, and keeper to frame ordinary dashboard concepts.
contribution: Contributes a compact mobile home-screen substrate with workshop-language metrics, activity-feed structure, summary plates, a persistent action, and icon navigation that a fused system could preserve as one ledger/dashboard expression.
summary_for_embedding: This is a v1 HTML mobile home screen mockup for counterpart-design showing workshop status metrics, daily summary plates, activity rows, a count strip, a primary action, and an icon rail.
intention_for_embedding: It was for exploring a ritualized workshop dashboard interface that turns ledger-like status and daily tasks into a tactile mobile screen.
